Sam Dillon (NYT)       Compiled by Christopher S. Wren

1998-10-27.title: A Jew, Then an Arab, Killed in the West Bank
1998-10-27.abstract: 28-year-old Jewish security guard is shot to death by two Palestinian gunmen in Hebron; 69-year-old Palestinian in slain in apparent act of revenge in Nablus; killings, linked to signing of Israeli-Palestinian peace accord in Washington, are seen by many as manifestation of inevitable spasm of violence that will dog peace effort, and to others as evidence that there is no genuine good will; maps; photo (M)

1998-10-27.title: South Africa Braces Itself For Report by Truth Panel
1998-10-27.abstract: South Africa's Truth and Reconciliation Commission, in report wrapping up two years of hearings into human-rights abuses during apartheid era, is expected to lay overwhelming blame on Government, but will also implicate African National Congress; some 200 people--including former Pres F W de Klerk and P W Botha, several current ANC cabinet members and Chief Mangosuthu Buthelezi, leader of Inkatha Freedom Party--have been notified that report will contain negative findings specifically about them; de Klerk says he will move to block report because of unfounded allegations; photo of commission chairman, Abp Desmond Tutu (M)

1998-10-27.title: New Actions On Pinochet Begin in Paris And Geneva
1998-10-27.abstract: Efforts begin in Geneva and Paris to have former Chilean dictator, Gen Augusto Pinochet, extradited to Spain to stand trial for genocide, even as his lawyers in London move to quash Spanish arrest warrant; his lawyers argue before High Court of Justice that Spanish arrest order is illegal since Pinochet, who has been under arrest at London clinic for past 10 days, is not Spanish citizen; lawyer for Spanish prosecutor defends warrant; says Pinochet was responsible for deaths of at least 4,000 people and had set out to destroy Chilean nationalists who did not share his ideological values (M)

1998-10-27.title: A Spy Mystery:Why Did Clinton Deny Israeli Plea?
1998-10-27.abstract: PARIS—  As long as it remains publicly unclear exactly what harm he did to the United States, it will be hard to understand exactly what made Jonathan Pollard, Israel's spy in the Pentagon, such a high-stakes issue that the U.S.-brokered accord last week between Israel and the Palestinians was nearly torpedoed.

1998-10-28.title: Mexico's Wanted Poster Exchanges Black Hat for White Collar
1998-10-28.abstract: Mexico's Congress is adorned with wanted posters bearing image of central bank governor and former Finance Min Guillermo Ortiz Martinez, whom many opposition lawmakers consider to be unrepentant white-collar outlaw and want impeached; he has become opposition target because, as Finance Minister after catastrophic devaluation of peso, he stitched together emergency program in 1995 that subsidized overwhelmed debtors and bought bad loans from failing banks; opposition has accused Government of seeking to rescue high-living bankers at expense of overburdened taxpayers (M)

1998-10-28.title: Era of Schroder and His Generation Begins
1998-10-28.abstract: Gerhard Schroeder, Social Democrat, becomes Germany's seventh postwar Chancellor and first member of his party to lead country since unification in 1990; is officially elected in emotional session by Parliament in 351-287 vote; is congratulated by outgoing Chancellor Helmut Kohl, who governed for 16 years; official transition comes month after Schroeder swept to victory in election that showed strong desire for change in Europe's most powerful state; photo (M)

1998-10-28.title: SERBIAN PULLOUTS LEAD NATO TO LIFT THREAT OF ATTACK
1998-10-28.abstract: NATO suspends immediate threat of air strikes against Yugoslavia, concluding that Pres Slobodan Milosevic is in substantial compliance with demands to end attacks against ethnic Albanians in Serbian province of Kosovo; agrees to extend indefinitely so-called activation order that authorizes use of force and keeps more than 400 warplanes on alert; Pres Clinton welcomes development; says NATO's willingness to act, combined with determined diplomacy, created chance to end suffering and repression in Kosovo; confusion still persists over exact number of troops remaining in Kosovo despite pullout since NATO eased initial demands on number of special paramilitary police officers it is prepared to allow to remain (M)

1998-10-28.title: ISRAELIS POSTPONE VOTE ON RATIFYING NEW PEACE ACCORD
1998-10-28.abstract: Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, under pressure from right-wingers, postpones ratification of new Israeli-Palestinian peace accord by Cabinet until Palestinians show plan to fight terrorism to US; postponement comes hours after Palestinian police report arrest of killers of Jewish settler, Danny Vargas, in Hebron--swift reponse seen as outcome of accord; postponement draws angry condemnations from Palestinian officials, who accuse Netanyahu of appeasing right-wing critics who have mounted fierce campaign against agreement and have vowed to unseat him; photo (M)

1998-10-28.title: Just Blunt, or a Bully? Canada Divided on Leader
1998-10-28.abstract: Article profiles Canada's Prime Min Jean Chretien, whose five years in office has been marked by directness uncommon among world leaders; at time of roundabout diplomacy and executive legalisms, he can be outspoken and blunt to degree that frightens and frustrates his handlers; photo (M)

1998-10-29.title: Peace Accord Leaves Israeli Labor Party in a Tough Spot Over Whether to Topple Netanyahu
1998-10-29.abstract: Israel's Labor Party, which has promised to back Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu against his former allies on right to protect his peace accord with Palestinians, balks at endorsing him or his budget; Netanyahu backs down, delaying budget; episode makes clear to many Labor members awkwardness of their current role; they are concerned that he could retain power in any new elections by cutting into centrist constituency and that election could disrupt peace effort (M)

1998-10-29.title: South Africa Truth Commission May Withhold Its Report
1998-10-29.abstract: South Africa's Truth and Reconciliation Commission is on verge of withholding its final report in face of court challenges from ex-Pres F W de Klerk and African National Congress, both apparently accused of gross human rights violations during apartheid era; Abp Desmond Tutu, panel chairman, is furious at last-minute tactics; says panel will reluctantly delete reference to de Klerk, but needs time to fight legal challenge; photo (M)

1998-10-29.title: Despite a Truce, Kosovo Albanians Dig New Graves and Vow Revenge
1998-10-29.abstract: Serb forces and ethnic Albanians in Kosovo, Serbia, fueled by deepening hatred, seek ways to continue fighting around truce demanded and enforced by West; Kosovo Liberation Army takes lead in burial of youth killed by booby trap left behind by Serb police, Dragobilje; vows revenge; photo (M)

1998-10-29.title: China Expert On Missiles Is Arrested On Return
1998-10-29.abstract: Stanford University colleagues say Hua Di, top Chinese missile scientist who has lived in United States since 1989, was arrested in Jan on his first trip home and awaits trial for allegedly revealing state secrets; he had been assured by Chinese security officials that he would not be arrested; American Embassy says it has raised issue with China; Stanford officials have protested to Pres Jiang Zemin; colleagues express concerns about his health (M)

1998-10-29.title: Iran Closes 'University' Run Covertly By the Bahais
1998-10-29.abstract: Iran, as part of crackdown on Bahai faith, its largest religious minority, has shut down stealth university where volunteer faculty was educating nearly 1,000 students; school was started in 1987 in reaction to virtual ban of Bahais from Iranian universities; operated so quietly that many Bahai officials abroad and many intellectuals within Iran were unaware of it (M)2

1998-10-30.title: Army Pullback in East Timor Disputed by Leaked Reports
1998-10-30.abstract: Opponents of Indonesia's control over disputed territory of East Timor say that documents leaked from Indonesian Ministry of Defense show that the army is not drawing down its forces in territory as Govt has reported (M)

1998-10-30.title: WORLD BRIEFING
1998-10-30.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: BLAIR AIDE FORCED TO QUIT -- The Government of Prime Minister Tony Blair has suffered its first forced resignation since taking office 18 months ago. Ron Davies is out as Secretary for Wales after he was robbed Monday night by strangers he invited into his car in an area known as a meeting place for homosexuals and drug dealers. Mr. Blair has often attacked the previous Conservative Government for the number of its members who left office in disgrace.   Warren Hoge (NYT)    BRITAIN: NEW VOTE PROCEDURES PROPOSED -- A commission headed by the former Finance Minister, Lord Jenkins, proposed new proportional voting procedures in elections for the House of Commons that would give small parties greater representation and make Continental-style coalition governments more likely. Prime Minister Tony Blair has promised a referendum on the current ''first-past-the-post'' electoral system, in which the winner takes all, sometimes even without a majority of votes.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

1998-10-30.title: Hong Kong Journal; For Fearfully Rich, a New Fad: A Fierce Gurkha
1998-10-30.abstract: Gurkha guards of Nepal, who long served in British Army in Hong Kong, but who found themselves relieved of duty when Britain handed Hong Kong over to China last year, have reinvented themselves as protectors of choice for Hong Kong's rich; business people, fearing wave of kidnappings, are hiring Gurkhas by the dozens to protect themselves and their families; photos (M)

1998-10-30.title: Israeli Jeep Blocks a Bomber From School Bus Carrying 40
1998-10-30.abstract: Jeep carrying Israeli soldiers intercepts Palestinian suicide bomber as he is about to ram car packed with explosives into school bus carrying children from Jewish settlement at Kfar Darom, Gaza; jeep absorbs explosion, which kills one soldier and wounds three others; bomber also dies; Yasir Arafat issues quick and unusually fierce condemnation of attack and then places under house arrest Sheik Ahmed Yassin, spiritual leader of Islamic fundamentalist group Hamas; drawing; photo; illus (M)

1998-10-30.title: Pretoria's Words: 'Extrajudicial Killing'
1998-10-30.abstract: Excerpts from final report issued by South Africa's Truth and Reconciliation Commission (M)

1998-10-31.title: Dora Maar's Picassos:A Trek Through Classicism and Caricature
1998-10-31.abstract: PARIS—  As visitors streamed into the Maison de la Chimie to view the Picasso memorabilia that sold this week for 205 million francs, some must have wondered whether the Paris School master had not played a phenomenal prank on society.

1998-10-31.title: C.I.A. Said to Reject Bomb Suspect's Bid to be Spy
1998-10-31.abstract: Ali A Mohamed, former sergeant in US Army who is being held in connection with terrorist campaign of Osama bin Laden, was rejected in 1980's when he offered himself to CIA as spy; CIA officials decided that Mohamed, who was officer in Egyptian military at time, was too unreliable to be useful agent inside Egypt (M)

1998-10-31.title: Wealthy Nations Back Plan To Speed Help to the Weak
1998-10-31.abstract: Leading industrial nations endorse Pres Clinton's proposal to help protect vulnerable but essentially healthy nations from currency and stock market upheavals that have battered nations from Asia and Latin America; proposal establishes new line of credit that would make billions of dollars available to some countries to ward off attacks on their currencies or markets that are set off by economic troubles elsewhere; plan, announced by Group of Seven industrial nations, offers new strategy for International Monetary Fund, which has usually offered money only after country is in deep economic trouble; plan was developed Treasury Sec Robert E Rubin and his aides (M)

1998-10-31.title: Security Council Outlines New Strategy on Iraq Arms
1998-10-31.abstract: Security Council outlines how it might proceed with comprehensive review of Iraq's relations with United Nations, if and when Pres Saddam Hussein allows arms inspections to resume; issue of Iraq's ban on spot inspections, imposed on Aug 5, and prospects for review, are likely to be discussed by Security Council with US Rep Peter Burleigh in president's chair for Nov (M)

1998-10-31.title: U.N. and World Bank Unite To Wage War on Malaria
1998-10-31.abstract: Three major United Nations agencies and World Bank are joining forces in coordinated campaign against malaria, leading killer of world's poorest people; project will be coordinated by World Health Organization in cooperation with UN Development Program, Unicef and World Bank; new drive is first project undertaken by WHO's new director general, Dr Gro Harlem Brundtland (M)
